seoTitle: How to Delegate Gmail Access in Google Workspace Safely
metaDescription: Learn how to delegate Gmail access in Google Workspace so an assistant or team member can manage your inbox securely without ever needing your password.
intro: Sharing inbox management with an assistant or team member doesn't have to mean handing over your password. This guide covers setting up Gmail delegation in Google Workspace through the Admin Console or directly from Gmail settings, so another user can manage emails on your behalf. It also explains how to remove a delegate later and what permissions delegated access actually includes.

steps.heading: Decide who needs delegated access
steps.body: Identify the assistant, manager, or team member who needs to manage your Gmail inbox without needing your login credentials.

steps.heading: Set up delegation via Admin Console or Gmail settings
steps.body: Grant delegated access either through the Google Workspace Admin Console or directly from the user's Gmail settings, depending on your setup.

steps.heading: Understand delegate permissions and limitations
steps.body: Review what a delegate can and can't do with the shared inbox, since delegated access comes with specific permission boundaries.

steps.heading: Remove a delegate when access is no longer needed
steps.body: Revoke delegated access once it's no longer needed to keep your inbox secure and limit who can manage it.

faq.question: Can I share my Gmail inbox without giving out my password?
faq.answer: Yes, Gmail delegation lets another user manage your inbox in Google Workspace without ever needing your login credentials.

faq.question: Who can set up Gmail delegation?
faq.answer: It can be set up through the Admin Console or directly from the individual user's Gmail settings.

faq.question: What can a delegate actually do in my inbox?
faq.answer: Delegated access comes with specific permissions and limitations, so a delegate's abilities are more restricted than full account access.

faq.question: How do I remove someone's delegated access?
faq.answer: You can revoke a delegate's access at any time through the same settings used to grant it originally.
